_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/5/26 3:00 PM: 
Good afternoon from Crystal Mountain! The early morning cloud coverage burned off and the sunshine came out. Things warmed up a bit which allowed for more edge grip and smooth turns. Big shout out to our grooming team for making an excellent carving canvas today. Don't forget you can still join in on the fun, as we have night skiing until 7pm tonight! 

Terrain Parks: The Discovery Park, Arwine's Jump Line and the new Sudden Drop Park (Lower Arwine's) are open today!

Events: On Sunday March 8th, join us for Get the Girls Out. This event cultivates a community for women to have fun, connect with new and old friends and creates opportunities.

Campbell Basin Rail Jam has moved to next Saturday March 14th!!!
From 11am-2pm throw down your cleanest lines, stomp your best tricks, and compete for some seriously sweet prizes from our stacked sponsors. Not competing? No stress — pull up, hype your crew, and catch all the action from Campbell Basin Lodge (elite viewing vibes included).


Notes for today:
  1. Summit House will be closed March 11 and Rafters will be closed March 11 and 12 for private events. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. Night Skiing hours have been adjusted to 4:00 p.m. to 7 p.m.
  4. We’ve decided to discontinue the parking waitlist for the 25/26 season. Parking reservations are back to first-come, first-served basis, and still required on weekends and holidays
  5. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  6. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  7. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  8. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  9. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/5/2026 4:20pm 

Forecaster: Bradford Swanson

Synopsis: The atmospheric pot continues to get stirred by a large, and I do mean large, high-pressure ridge that extends the entire length of the West coast. This ridge is stirring up localized disturbances in our region, delivering a small moisture blip overnight and into Friday. On Friday, we can expect warming temps, light precip, and moderate winds. Friday night is when things start to get a little bit more tropical. As the ridge collapses, so does our influx of cold northern air, sending freezing levels sky-high. Thankfully, the fire under this cauldron is more of a flash in the pan and not a proper inferno. More on this below. 

Thursday Night:
Sky Cover: Cloudy
Freezing Elevation: 4,000’
12hr Precip: Trace amounts possible
Wind Trends: Moderate, West
Friday:
Sky Cover: Cloudy
Freezing Elevation: 5,500’
12hr Precip: Trace - 2”
Wind Trends: Light to Moderate, West
Friday Night:
Sky Cover: Cloudy
Freezing Elevation: 10,500’
12hr Precip: Trace amounts possible
Wind Trends: Strong to Extreme West Northwest, gusting 60+

Extended: Like waking a sleeping baby up from a deep slumber, Saturday is looking to be quite loud and cranky. As the temps continue to rise, so do the winds, with potential gusts exceeding 60mph throughout the day. It could be a little western out on the ridges, but the lower-elevation skiing should be sheltered from its wrath. As Sunday rolls around, the cranky weather should subside, freezing levels should drop, and the wind will slowly calm as we approach the start of the week. 

*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F










Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification
